agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering expedited courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and conditional refund policy. Currently in product discovery with unresolved stakeholder conflicts around the nature of the delivery promise.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-constrained delivery with premium pricing, driver eligibility rules, and a distinct refund/SLA policy separate from standard delivery.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available only to eligible customers within a defined radius of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge added on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects express delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery speed. Never refunded under the express SLA." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period from order placement within which the express delivery is targeted for completion." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute internal operational target for a driver to pick up an express order from the warehouse after placement." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Radius", "definition": "The 10km maximum distance from a warehouse to the customer's delivery address required for express eligibility." },
      { "term": "Driver Eligibility (Express)", "definition": "A driver must hold a rating of at least 4.0 stars to be assigned express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "When an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering the refund policy." },
      { "term": "Replacement Assignment Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a mid-delivery driver cancellation, or an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exception", "definition": "A refund exclusion that applies when the delivery failure is attributable to incorrect address information provided by the customer." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical/logistical constraint. Offering express outside this radius would make the 2-hour target physically impossible and corrupt the eligibility model.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address >10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option must not appear as selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by leadership. The fee amount is not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "An express order's total delivery charge equals base_delivery_fee + $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 15-minute pickup window is an operational target/policy. It could be adjusted without corrupting system state, though it affects the feasibility of the 2-hour window.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up an express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate (alert ops, attempt reassignment, or flag the order as at-risk)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible for express delivery assignments.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Driver rating threshold is a quality policy that a VP could adjust. Not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must never assign an express order to a driver whose current rating is below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an express delivery misses the 2-hour window, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded, but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This defines the refund scope and protects against over-refunding. Refunding the base fee or failing to refund the express fee would violate the financial contract with the customer. This is a data consistency rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "A missed-window refund transaction must equal exactly the express fee amount, never more, never less."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is an operational SLA, not a system integrity constraint. Could be changed by finance policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "A refund triggered by a missed window must have a processing deadline set to 3 business days from the miss event."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the missed window was caused by the customer providing an incorrect address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a fault-attribution rule that determines financial liability. Issuing a refund when the fault lies with the customer would corrupt the refund integrity model.",
      "testable_assertion": "If an express delivery is flagged as 'wrong_address', no express fee refund is created regardless of whether the window was missed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute replacement window is an operational policy. The auto-refund trigger is a business decision that could be adjusted.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an active express delivery, if no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, the system must automatically initiate an express fee refund without human intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express customers must have access to real-time driver tracking from pickup to delivery.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time tracking is a product feature requirement, not a system integrity constraint. The delivery can still complete without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "From the moment a driver picks up an express order, the customer-facing app must display live driver location updates at intervals of no more than [TBD] seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — need to clarify whether the 10km radius is inclusive (<=10km) or exclusive (<10km). Recommend inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars mid-delivery on an express order", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should the driver complete the current delivery? Almost certainly yes, but the rule as stated doesn't address in-flight orders." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a correct but hard-to-find address (e.g., new construction, gated community)", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — the 'wrong address' exception needs a clear definition. Is a correct-but-difficult address the customer's fault? Who adjudicates?" },
    { "scenario": "No eligible drivers (4+ stars) are available within the 15-minute pickup window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should the system lower the rating threshold, cancel express and downgrade to standard, or refund proactively?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels within the 10-minute replacement window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does the 10-minute clock reset? Or does the total elapsed time since first cancellation apply?" },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:50 PM — the 2-hour window spans midnight and potentially a timezone boundary", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — how are business days calculated for refund processing? Does the 2-hour window account for warehouse operating hours?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement but before pickup", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — if the new address is >10km from warehouse, does express eligibility get re-evaluated? Is the delivery window reset?" },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse goes offline (outage, emergency) after express order is placed but before pickup", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — who bears the fault? Should auto-refund trigger immediately?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills the order?", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — warehouse selection logic is not specified. Nearest? Best-stocked? Shortest estimated pickup time?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream, but the refund policy creates downside risk. If missed-window rates are high, net revenue could be negative after refund processing costs. The 'wrong address' exclusion protects against customer-fault refunds but needs a clear adjudication process to avoid disputes and chargebacks.",
    "operational": "Significant operational strain: 15-minute pickup SLA requires pre-positioned or dedicated express drivers. The 4-star rating requirement shrinks the eligible driver pool. The 10-minute replacement window demands real-time dispatch optimization. All three constraints compound — if the eligible driver pool is thin, all SLAs become harder to meet.",
    "user": "Strong positive UX signal: real-time tracking + 2-hour window + prominent guarantee messaging builds trust. However, the Legal/PM contradiction on whether this is a 'guarantee' creates a messaging risk — if customers perceive a guarantee that Legal won't honor, trust erodes on first failure.",
    "risk": "HIGH — the contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort goodwill') is the single biggest risk. If the product launches with 'guaranteed' language and Legal refuses to honor refunds consistently, customer trust collapses. Secondary risk: the wrong-address adjudication process is undefined and will generate customer disputes from day one.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with time-sensitive delivery promise. High expectations set by 'guarantee' language. Refund disputes likely on wrong-address edge cases." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "New eligibility tier creates a two-class driver system. 4-star threshold incentivizes rating maintenance but may feel exclusionary. Express assignments likely carry higher pressure (15-min pickup, real-time tracking visibility)." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must prioritize express orders for sub-15-minute handoff. May require dedicated express staging areas or workflows." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New refund category with 3-business-day processing SLA. Must build or adapt refund pipeline. Need fraud/abuse monitoring for the refund policy." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will bear the brunt of disputes around wrong-address exclusions, missed windows, and the guarantee-vs-best-effort confusion. Need clear escalation playbooks." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must approve all customer-facing language. The 'guarantee' framing is a liability risk they have explicitly flagged." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is low due to a fundamental contradiction between PM and Legal on the core product promise (guarantee vs. best effort), which cascades into every downstream rule. Additionally, multiple critical operational flows are undefined (wrong-address adjudication, no-driver-available fallback, replacement cancellation chaining). The spec is a raw workshop transcript, not a refined product requirements document.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) — explicit and uncontested",
      "Warehouse radius eligibility (10km) — explicit and uncontested",
      "Driver rating threshold (4.0 stars) — explicit and uncontested",
      "Refund scope: express fee only, not base fee — explicit and uncontested",
      "Real-time tracking requirement — explicit and uncontested"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Nature of the delivery promise: guarantee vs. best-effort — directly contradicted between PM and Legal",
      "Wrong-address adjudication process — no definition of who decides or what evidence is required",
      "Behavior when operational SLAs cannot be met (no drivers, no pickup in 15 min) — undefined fallback",
      "Replacement driver cascading failure — undefined",
      "Interaction between 15-minute pickup SLA and 2-hour delivery window — is the window from placement or from pickup?"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts from order placement, not from pickup (PM said 'within 2 hours' without qualification)",
      "The 10km radius is measured as straight-line distance, not driving distance — but this is not specified",
      "The 4-star rating is checked at assignment time, not continuously during delivery",
      "The 3-business-day refund processing window is a maximum, not a target",
      "Express orders use the same inventory as standard orders — no dedicated express inventory was mentioned"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states express delivery is 'guaranteed within 2 hours' and promises refunds for missed windows. Legal explicitly states this cannot be a guarantee — it is 'best effort' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are irreconcilable: either we promise and must honor, or we don't promise and refunds are discretionary. This affects every customer-facing message, every refund rule, and every support interaction.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Product Management"],
      "options": [
        "A: Market as 'guarantee' with mandatory refund — accept legal risk, build refund automation",
        "B: Market as 'Express Target' (best effort) with discretionary goodwill refunds — reduces legal risk but weakens product positioning",
        "C: Market as 'Express Promise' with automatic refund as a policy (not contractual obligation) — middle ground requiring careful legal language"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — position the refund as company policy rather than contractual guarantee. Use language like 'Our Express Promise: we target 2-hour delivery. If we miss it, we refund the express fee — that's our policy.' This satisfies Legal (not a legal guarantee) while giving PM a strong customer-facing commitment. BUT — this requires explicit sign-off from both PM and Legal before any acceptance criteria can be finalized."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' refund exclusion has no defined adjudication process. Who determines whether an address was 'wrong'? The driver? An automated system? Customer support? What constitutes 'wrong' — typo, missing apartment number, outdated address, correct but hard to find? This will be the #1 source of customer disputes.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address in app, customer support adjudicates with evidence",
        "B: Automated address validation at order time — reject unverifiable addresses before express is offered",
        "C: Hybrid — validate at order time AND allow driver to flag, with support as final arbiter"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — validate addresses upfront to prevent most issues, but retain a driver-flag + support-adjudication path for edge cases. Define 'wrong address' explicitly: an address that does not correspond to a real, accessible delivery location as provided by the customer."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No fallback behavior is defined for when operational constraints cannot be met: (1) No 4-star+ driver available within 15 minutes, (2) Replacement driver also cancels, (3) Warehouse cannot fulfill within pickup SLA. These are not rare edge cases — they will happen daily at scale.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Drivers", "Warehouse Operations", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-downgrade to standard delivery with proactive customer notification and express fee refund",
        "B: Extend the search/lower thresholds temporarily (e.g., accept 3.5-star drivers as fallback)",
        "C: Cancel the express component and let customer choose: wait for standard or cancel entirely"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-downgrade with immediate express fee refund and proactive notification. This preserves customer trust, avoids silent failures, and keeps the standard delivery intact. But this needs PM + Ops sign-off."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 2-hour delivery window start time is ambiguous. PM says 'within 2 hours' (implying from placement). Ops says pickup must happen within 15 minutes (implying the clock starts at placement). But if the window is from placement, and pickup takes 15 of those 120 minutes, the actual transit time is 105 minutes for up to 10km — feasible but tight. Need explicit confirmation: does the 2-hour clock start at order placement, order confirmation, or driver pickup?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Drivers", "Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Clock starts at order placement (customer expectation)",
        "B: Clock starts at driver pickup (operational reality)",
        "C: Clock starts at order placement, displayed to customer; internal SLA tracks pickup + transit separately"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with Option C's internal tracking — the customer sees '2 hours from now' (their expectation), while internally ops tracks pickup-SLA and transit-SLA as separate metrics. This is the only interpretation that matches what a customer would understand."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define acceptance criteria due to unresolved contradiction between PM and Legal on the nature of the delivery promise (guarantee vs. best effort). This is a foundational product definition issue that must be resolved before any implementation begins.", "business_rule_broken": "The core delivery promise — which drives refund logic (BR-5, BR-8), customer-facing messaging, and support processes — is contradicted by two authoritative stakeholders." }
  ]
}
